title: Allergy Management Tips
heroTitle: A tool that turns today's symptoms into an immediate plan
description: How to protect your health on days with high pollen and fine dust. Select your symptoms for personalized advice.

sections.heading: High / Very High Pollen
tips: Stay indoors as much as possible
Wear an N95 or FFP2 mask when going outside
Keep windows closed, especially in the early morning (5-10 AM)
Shower and wash your face immediately after returning home
Remove outdoor clothes at the door and wash them promptly
Take allergy medication 30 minutes before going outside

sections.heading: Moderate Pollen
tips: Sensitive individuals should consider wearing a mask
Avoid prolonged outdoor activity
Wash hands thoroughly when returning home
Limit morning ventilation when pollen counts tend to be higher

sections.heading: Low Pollen
tips: A great day for outdoor activities
Brief morning ventilation is fine

sections.heading: Poor Air Quality
tips: Wear an N95 or FFP2 mask outdoors
Run an air purifier indoors
Avoid strenuous outdoor exercise
Eat antioxidant-rich foods (fruits and vegetables)

sections.heading: Indoor Management
tips: Choose houseplants that do not produce pollen
Wash bedding at least once a week at 60°C or higher
Prefer damp mopping over vacuuming to avoid stirring up dust
Maintain indoor humidity at 40-50% (too dry worsens symptoms)
Be aware of pet dander allergies
